input: What is multi-head attention and why does the Transformer use it?
actual_output: Multi-head attention is a mechanism that allows the model to jointly attend to information from different representation subspaces at different positions. The Transformer uses multi-head attention in three different ways, including in "encoder-decoder attention" layers, to allow the model to attend to information from different subspaces. This is necessary because a single attention head would inhibit this ability.
